title: GitHub Review by berendt
summary: The reviewer found one critical issue: the `pull_request` trigger in `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` is missing `types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]`, which means the `run-chaos` label feature will never fire when a label is added to an existing PR. There are also 7 warnings, the most notable being an undocumented `architecture` submodule bump that should either be explained in the PR description or split into a separate PR.
external_feedback:
> Reviewed by planwerk-review 3a5c8bd-dirty with Claude CLI

<!-- planwerk-review: blocking=0 critical=1 warning=7 info=2 recommendation=CUSTOM -->

## CRITICAL (1)

### C-001: run-chaos label trigger will not fire when label is added to existing PR
**File**: `.github/workflows/ci.yaml:7-12` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Guard event-type-specific fields in cross-event workflows

**Problem**: The workflow's on.pull_request trigger does not include types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]. By default, GitHub Actions only triggers pull_request on opened, synchronize, and reopened — not labeled. Adding the run-chaos label to an existing PR will not trigger a workflow run, making the entire run-chaos label feature (REQ-007) non-functional.

**Code**:
```
"on":
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["v*"]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
```

**Action Required**: Add types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled] to the pull_request trigger.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
"on":
  push:
    branches: [main]
    tags: ["v*"]
  pull_request:
    branches: [main]
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened, labeled]
```

---

## WARNING (7)

### W-001: Scope Creep: Unlisted architecture submodule bump
**File**: `architecture:1` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: The architecture submodule is bumped from 7beebd6 to d58b67d but the PR description does not mention architecture doc changes. The submodule bump is unlisted and its contents cannot be reviewed in this diff.

**Code**:
```
-Subproject commit 7beebd6f2d66a1012f16a52c3990bed9033b5af8
+Subproject commit d58b67d87c9f45a48fc2d5a285a09423ec20ecde
```

**Action Required**: Either document the architecture submodule change in the PR description or split it into a separate PR if it is unrelated to the network chaos feature.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add the architecture submodule bump rationale to the PR description, or revert it if unrelated to this feature.
```

---

### W-002: Stale Documentation: ci-workflow.md e2e-chaos job details outdated
**File**: `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md:473-525` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Update documentation when referenced code behavior changes

**Problem**: The CI workflow documentation is stale in multiple places: (1) Line 480 lists e2e-operator in dependencies but it was removed, (2) Line 481 doesn't mention run-chaos label trigger, (3) Lines 483-488 claim e2e-chaos depends on e2e-operator, (4) Line 511 shows 60 minute timeout but actual is 45 minutes, (5) Line 513 shows e2e-operator dependency, (6) Line 477 lists only 3 scenarios, missing network partition and network latency.

**Code**:
```
**Dependencies:** `needs: [changes, lint, shellcheck, test, test-integration, verify-codegen, e2e-operator]`
**Condition:** Runs only when `e2e-chaos == 'true'` and no dependency failed or was cancelled.
```

**Action Required**: Update docs/reference/ci-workflow.md to reflect: removed e2e-operator dependency, added run-chaos label trigger, timeout changed to 45 minutes, updated test suite list to include network partition and network latency, updated DAG diagram.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Update the following in docs/reference/ci-workflow.md:
- Line 480: Remove e2e-operator from dependencies list
- Line 481: Add 'or when the run-chaos label is present on a pull request'
- Lines 483-488: Remove mention of e2e-operator dependency
- Line 511: Change timeout from 60 to 45 minutes
- Line 513: Remove e2e-operator from dependencies
- Line 477: Add 'Network partition' and 'Network latency' to the list
```

**Related**: W-003, W-004

---

### W-003: Stale Documentation: ci-workflow.md DAG diagram shows stale e2e-chaos dependencies
**File**: `docs/reference/ci-workflow.md:101` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Verify documentation headings match actual dependency graph

**Problem**: The DAG diagram at line 101 shows e2e-chaos depending on e2e-operator, but the actual workflow no longer has this dependency.

**Code**:
```
  e2e-chaos ──────> needs: [changes, lint, shellcheck, test, test-integration, verify-codegen, e2e-operator]
```

**Action Required**: Remove e2e-operator from the DAG diagram line for e2e-chaos.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
  e2e-chaos ──────> needs: [changes, lint, shellcheck, test, test-integration, verify-codegen]
```

**Related**: W-002

### W-004: Stale Documentation: chaos-e2e-tests.md has no reference to network chaos scenarios
**File**: `docs/reference/chaos-e2e-tests.md:9` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified — **Pattern**: Cross-reference documentation examples against implementation

**Problem**: The chaos E2E test documentation has no mention of the two new network chaos scenarios (SC-CHAOS-006 mariadb-network-partition, SC-CHAOS-007 mariadb-network-latency). Every existing scenario has a corresponding section with step table, catch block documentation, and CR fixture entry. Specific gaps: (1) Line 9 frontmatter missing CC-0049, (2) Line 19 says '7 chaos test suites' instead of 9, (3) Lines 24-25 only mention PodChaos not NetworkChaos, (4) Lines 28-69 ASCII diagram missing network chaos phase, (5) Lines 67-68 only mention PodChaos CRDs, (6) Line 74 says '7 test suites', (7) Lines 149-157 summary table missing new rows, (8) Lines 653-665 CR Fixtures table missing new entries, (9) Lines 699-731 file layout tree missing new directories.

**Code**:
```
The 7 chaos test suites validate operator behavior during and after fault injection.
```

**Action Required**: Add documentation sections for both network chaos scenarios following the established pattern, update all counts from 7 to 9, add CR fixture table entries, update file layout tree, and update PodChaos-only references to include NetworkChaos.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Comprehensive documentation update required: add full scenario sections for SC-CHAOS-006 and SC-CHAOS-007 following the sibling pattern (step table, catch blocks, design notes), update counts 7→9 at lines 19 and 74, add NetworkChaos to lines 24-25 and 67-68, add rows to summary table at lines 149-157, add CR fixture entries at lines 653-665, add directory entries at lines 699-731, and add CC-0049 to frontmatter feature list at line 9.
```

**Related**: W-002

### W-005: Network partition test does not assert fault injection is active before checking effects
**File**: `tests/e2e-chaos/mariadb-network-partition/chainsaw-test.yaml:47-56` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: uncertain — **Pattern**: Assert absence of old state in mutation tests

**Problem**: The mariadb-network-partition test Step 4 asserts DatabaseReady=False to confirm the partition took effect, but there is no mechanism to confirm the partition actually disrupted anything. If the Chaos Mesh selector doesn't match (e.g., label mismatch), the NetworkChaos CR is accepted but has no effect. DatabaseReady might briefly flicker False for an unrelated reason, and the test passes vacuously.

**Code**:
```
  - try:
    - assert:
        resource:
          apiVersion: keystone.openstack.c5c3.io/v1alpha1
          kind: Keystone
          metadata:
            name: keystone-chaos-net-part
            namespace: openstack
          status:
            (conditions[?type == 'DatabaseReady']):
            - status: "False"
```

**Action Required**: Add an assertion in Step 4 that the NetworkChaos CR is in an Injected/Applied state before asserting the condition change, or add a script step that verifies the NetworkChaos CR status shows the fault is active.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Add before the DatabaseReady assertion:
    - assert:
        resource:
          apiVersion: chaos-mesh.org/v1alpha1
          kind: NetworkChaos
          metadata:
            name: partition-mariadb
          status:
            conditions:
            - type: AllInjected
              status: "True"
Note: verify the exact Chaos Mesh NetworkChaos status condition schema before applying.
```

**Related**: W-006, W-007

---

### W-006: Network latency test uses sleep 30 instead of condition-based wait
**File**: `tests/e2e-chaos/mariadb-network-latency/chainsaw-test.yaml:63-65` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: likely — **Pattern**: Replace fixed sleeps with condition-based waits in tests

**Problem**: The latency test Step 4 uses sleep 30 to wait for latency to affect reconciliation. This is fragile — on slow CI/clusters, 30s may not be enough for a full reconciliation cycle with 10s injected latency. On fast clusters, it wastes 30s of CI time. This is inconsistent with the project pattern of using condition-based waits where possible.

**Code**:
```
          # Allow latency to affect at least one reconciliation cycle.
          sleep 30
```

**Action Required**: Replace the fixed sleep with a poll for evidence that the latency is affecting the system — for example, poll the NetworkChaos CR status to confirm injection is active. Alternatively, document why 30s is the minimum and add CI timing considerations.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Replace the fixed sleep with a condition-based wait that polls NetworkChaos CR injection status, or at minimum document the 30s rationale with respect to the operator's reconciliation interval and the 10s injected latency.
```

**Related**: W-005

---

### W-007: Timeout budget for network partition test may be insufficient
**File**: `tests/e2e-chaos/mariadb-network-partition/01-networkchaos.yaml:33-34` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: uncertain — **Pattern**: Test script timeout must exceed worst-case loop duration

**Problem**: The NetworkChaos duration is 120s, which is the auto-expiry safety net. If the Step 4 assertion takes close to 120s to observe DatabaseReady=False, the auto-expiry could heal the partition before the test proceeds to Step 5 (explicit CR deletion). The latency test uses 180s for a less severe fault. The existing mariadb-pod-kill test uses PodChaos which has no duration-based auto-expiry.

**Code**:
```
  duration: "120s"
```

**Action Required**: Consider increasing the NetworkChaos duration to 300s (matching the assert timeout) to ensure the partition persists through the full assertion window.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Increase the NetworkChaos duration to 300s to match the assert timeout:
  duration: "300s"
```

**Related**: W-005

---

## INFO (2)

### I-001: Plan and chore commits mixed with feature work
**File**: `.planwerk/:1` — **Fix**: ASK — **Confidence**: verified

**Problem**: Commits 0862f04 and 179a6ed (plan persistence), e51714b (PR link), and 3f041e3 (feature start) are project management artifacts. The actual feature work is in a96f555 and the review fix in a883733.

**Code**:
```
0862f04 plan persistence
179a6ed plan persistence
e51714b PR link
3f041e3 feature start
a96f555 actual feature work
a883733 review fix
```

**Action Required**: No action required — consistent with the project's workflow pattern using .planwerk/. Consider squash-merging if a clean history is preferred.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
Consider squash-merging to consolidate project management commits with feature work, if clean history is desired.
```

---

### I-002: Network latency restart check only inspects containerStatuses[0]
**File**: `tests/e2e-chaos/mariadb-network-latency/chainsaw-test.yaml:87-89` — **Fix**: AUTO-FIX — **Confidence**: uncertain — **Pattern**: Avoid brittle index-based container assertions in tests

**Problem**: The restart count check uses containerStatuses[0].restartCount, which only checks the first container. If the operator pod ever gains a sidecar or init container with restartPolicy: Always, the assertion would check the wrong container. The current operator has a single container so [0] is correct today.

**Code**:
```
            RESTARTS=$(kubectl get pod -n "$OPERATOR_NS" "$POD" \
              -o jsonpath='{.status.containerStatuses[0].restartCount}')
```

**Action Required**: Use a jsonpath that finds the container by name instead of by index.

**Suggested Fix**:
```
            RESTARTS=$(kubectl get pod -n "$OPERATOR_NS" "$POD" \
              -o jsonpath='{.status.containerStatuses[?(@.name=="manager")].restartCount}')
```

---

## Summary

The PR delivers well-structured network chaos test scenarios that follow established project patterns. Test structure, CR fixtures, diagnostics integration, and CI job modifications are clean and consistent with the 7 existing test suites. The most significant issues are: (1) the run-chaos label trigger is non-functional because pull_request lacks types: [labeled], silently defeating the on-demand chaos feature; (2) multiple documentation files are stale — ci-workflow.md still documents the old dependency chain and timeout, and chaos-e2e-tests.md has no mention of the two new scenarios despite having entries for all existing ones; (3) several test robustness improvements are worth discussing, particularly around asserting fault injection state and replacing fixed sleeps.

| Severity | Count |
|----------|-------|
| BLOCKING | 0 |
| CRITICAL | 1 |
| WARNING  | 7 |
| INFO     | 2 |

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Recommendation**: Do not merge until F-001 (label trigger) is fixed — this is a silent feature breakage. The documentation staleness findings (F-002 through F-004) should also be addressed before merge to maintain the project's documentation standards. The test robustness findings (F-005, F-006, F-009) warrant discussion but could be addressed in a follow-up if the team prefers.
